untranslatable_reason: Signal requires external macroeconomic data (CPI YoY, 10Y yield, PMI, unemployment, credit conditions) that are NOT available in the ALLOWED INDICATORS list. These are real-time economic releases, not price-based technical indicators. The signal explicitly rejects pattern recognition and formulas in favor of fundamental economic analysis. Translation to price-action-only indicators would contradict the signal's core thesis.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_1 is a philosophical macro framework, not a mechanical trading rule. It emphasizes building conviction through observable economic variables (inflation, rates, growth data) and explicitly warns against self-destructing formulas. The entry conditions require: (1) CPI YoY > 3.5%, (2) 10Y yield move ±35 bps, (3) PMI/unemployment/credit direction shifts. None of these can be replicated from price data alone. To implement this signal, the backtest engine would need to integrate external macroeconomic data feeds (FRED, Bloomberg, etc.) rather than relying solely on OHLCV indicators. The 12% drawdown exit and 2-of-3 indicator threshold are translatable in principle, but without the macro inputs, entry conditions remain untranslatable.
